mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity, which drives higher corporate earnings and supports equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO level changes on 60-day forward returns is not positive over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal produces no significant excess returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If, in periods when UNRATE is above 5%, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and decline in housing starts indicates that monetary tightening is effectively cooling housing demand, a leading indicator of economic slowdown, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * -HOUST) has a rank IC of zero or positive over any 3-year window, or the signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in periods when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4%, further increases are more likely to contract economic activity and depress equity prices, as the economy is already in a restrictive monetary environment.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 4%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns is positive or not significantly negative (p>0.05) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, which reduces revenue for retailers and consumer goods companies, driving stock prices lower over the following month.
failure_condition: In months with negative RSAFS growth, the S&P 500 20-day return is not negative on average (t-test fails) or positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2%, it signals overheating that prompts the Fed to tighten policy, which increases discount rates and depresses equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After CPI YoY exceeds 2%, the 60-day forward return of SPX is not negative on average, or the signal fails to improve upon a buy-and-hold strategy by 5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and payroll employment confirm a synchronous economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The combined signal (both series rising) does not produce a higher average 20-day return than when only one rises, or the interaction term rank IC is negative over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Following a quarter of negative GDP growth, a return to positive growth signals the start of a recovery phase, which historically leads to strong equity gains over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth becomes positive after a negative quarter
failure_condition: After the trigger (positive GDP growth following negative quarter), the 60-day SPX return is negative on average or less than the unconditional average return over the same periods.
